title: MPPSC 2026 — MP State Services Exam
meta_description: MPPSC 2026 — Madhya Pradesh PSC exam for DSP, Naib Tehsildar, BDO. Prelims/Mains pattern, MP-specific syllabus, salary ₹36K-1.5L.

hero.one_liner: Madhya Pradesh's state civil services exam — gateway to DSP, Naib Tehsildar, BDO, and Group A/B administrative positions.

what_is_it: MPPSC (Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission) State Services Examination (SSE) recruits officers for Madhya Pradesh administrative posts: Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP), Naib Tehsildar (revenue), Block Development Officer (BDO), Assistant Director (various departments). Three stages: Prelims (Paper 1: GS 100 Qs, Paper 2: CSAT 100 Qs qualifying), Mains (6 papers totaling 1400 marks), and Interview. MPPSC notification released December 31, 2025, with 155 vacancies for various positions.

MPPSC 2026 Prelims is on April 26, 2026, with application window January 10-February 9, 2026. Notably, MPPSC changed exam pattern: Each question now carries 3 marks (earlier: 1 mark), and negative marking introduced (1 mark per wrong answer). This changes strategy significantly — careless errors are costlier. Mains is scheduled September 7-12, 2026. MP-specific content (20-30% of Prelims GS) tests: MP history (tribal movements, princely states), geography (Narmada, rivers), economy (soybean, agriculture), and current state affairs.

DSP posts at Level 10 (₹56,100 basic) offer ₹80,000-1,00,000/month with DA/allowances. Naib Tehsildar at Level 8 (₹47,600) offers ₹55,000-70,000/month. BDO positions offer similar salary at Level 8-10. MPPSC is moderately easier than UPSC but more difficult than some state PSCs due to increased competition and MP-specific content requirement. Successful candidates prepare 8-12 months with balanced focus on national GS and MP GK.

MPPSC applicants: 4+ lakh for 155-500 vacancies (0.05-0.1% selection rate). Bhopal and Indore are coaching hubs. Unlike UPSC which many reject candidates also attempt, MPPSC attracts serious state-focused aspirants. Advantage: Fewer UPSC toppers compete here, making it relatively more achievable. Disadvantage: MP-specific content cannot be ignored.

exam_pattern.description: 100 MCQs, each 3 marks. Total 300 marks. 2 hours duration. Heavy MP content (25-30%). Negative marking: -1 per wrong.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: -1 for each wrong answer. +3 for correct.

exam_pattern.description: 100 MCQs, each 3 marks. Total 300 marks. Qualifying exam (need 33% to pass). Does not count for ranking.

extra_sections.heading: MP-Specific Topics — Exam-Critical Preparation
content:
MP History (15-20% of Prelims GS)

Tribal uprisings: Bhil rebellion, Gond rebellions. Bundela Rajput history and kingdoms. 1857 revolt in MP: Tantya Tope's pivotal role (guerrilla warfare), Rani Durgavati's resistance.
Princely states of Madhya Bharat. Formation of modern MP (1956 from integration of various regions).

Separation of Chhattisgarh (2000) — biggest administrative change. Questions: 'Tantya Tope's real name?' 'Rani Durgavati's kingdom?' 'Year of Chhattisgarh separation?' — memorize exact details from standard histories.

MP Geography (15-20% of Prelims GS)

Narmada river: Sacred river, valley civilization, Narmada Valley Project, dam controversy. Satpura and Vindhya mountain ranges.
Malwa Plateau. Major dams: Sardar Sarovar (Gujarat-MP joint), Bargi (Jabalpur region), Tawa, Bhadbhada.
Forests: Sal forests, bamboo zones, forest-dependent tribal populations. Wildlife sanctuaries: Kanha National Park (tigers), Bandhavgarh National Park, Panna (reintroduction of cheetahs).
Soil types: Black soil in southern MP (good for cotton), red soil in eastern regions. Agricultural zones: Cotton belt (Indore), wheat zones.

MP Economy & Current State Affairs

Agriculture: Soybean production (India's capital), wheat, rice, cotton. Minerals: Diamonds (Panna), bauxite, copper.
Industries: Cement, steel, power generation. Tourism: Khajuraho temples (UNESCO), Sanchi Stupa, Gwalior Fort.
Major schemes: Ladli Behna Yojana (women support), Sikho Kamao (skill development), Ladli Laxmi (girl child welfare). State government: Chief Minister, recent policy initiatives.
Budget allocations, infrastructure development, e-governance projects. Follow Dainik Bhaskar/Patrika (Hindi newspapers) for current affairs.

extra_sections.heading: MPPSC Prelims Strategy — New 3-Mark Pattern Impact
content:
3-Mark Pattern Changes Everything

Previously, 1 mark correct and -1/3 negative meant: Attempt uncertain questions if 75%+ confident. Now, 3 marks correct and -1 negative means: Only attempt if 90%+ confident.
This shifts strategy drastically. With 100 questions: Attempt 60 high-confidence Qs carefully (54 marks).
Skip 30 medium-confidence Qs. Attempt 10 educated-guess Qs.
Result: 54 + 3 = 57 out of 300 = 19% score. Cutoff for MPPSC Prelims is typically 90-120 marks out of 300 (30-40%), which translates to attempting only 45-60 questions accurately.
This is NOT about attempting all 100.

Paper 1 (GS) vs Paper 2 (CSAT)

Paper 1 counts for ranking. Paper 2 is just qualifying (need 33%).
Strategy: Invest 80% effort in Paper 1 GS preparation, 20% on CSAT. In exam: Spend 1.5 hours careful on Paper 1, 30 minutes on Paper 2 (just clear cutoff).
CSAT is general aptitude — usually easier to cross 33% with basic math + reasoning. GS is where differentiation happens.

preparation_tips: MPPSC Prelims GS paper tests factual knowledge more than conceptual frameworks. Focus on facts: exact numbers (MP population, literacy rate, forest cover %), major production figures (soybean tons/year), geographical coordinates, historical dates. Read standard GS books (Lucent, Manorama Yearbook) + MP-specific materials (Sanjeev Verma's MP GK, government yearbooks).

Hindi language proficiency is scoring in MPPSC Mains. Both Hindi language paper and Hindi essay carry significant marks. Most MP candidates are comfortable in Hindi — use this advantage. Write practice essays in Hindi weekly from month 4. If Hindi is weak, dedicate 3 months to Hindi grammar, comprehension, and essay writing.

MPPSC conducts exams at irregular intervals — sometimes 2 exams in one year, sometimes gaps. Stay prepared year-round rather than targeting specific dates. If you miss this 2026 notification, another might come within 6-12 months. Keep revision ongoing.

Join MPPSC-specific coaching if possible. Bhopal and Indore centers (like Sanjeev Verma's institute, other local coaching) offer MP GK modules. For self-study, follow: mppsc.com for notifications, Sanjeev Verma sir's YouTube channel for MP GK, join MPPSC Telegram groups for peer discussions and current affairs sharing.

Solve previous year MPPSC papers (2015-2025, 10 years) multiple times. Question patterns repeat. Some exact questions appear across years with slight variations. After 3-4 complete revisions of previous papers, you'll recognize 15-20% of actual exam questions.

faqs.question: MPPSC vs UPPSC — which is easier and which to choose?
faqs.answer: Vacancy-wise: MPPSC (155-500 seats) vs UPPSC (200-400 seats) similar. Competition per seat: Similar (4L+ applicants). Difficulty-wise: Similar. Question patterns: MPPSC more factual, UPPSC more conceptual. Choose based on: If Madhya Pradesh home state, choose MPPSC (advantage in MP GK). If Uttar Pradesh home state, choose UPPSC. Both are equally achievable. Many candidates from one state appear for both.

faqs.question: Can non-MP residents apply for MPPSC?
faqs.answer: Yes. MPPSC is open to all Indian citizens. No domicile requirement for most general category posts. Some specific reserved category posts may require MP domicile — check detailed notification. General category applicants from any state can compete.

faqs.question: DSP vs Naib Tehsildar — which is better?
faqs.answer: DSP: Level 10 (higher pay), dynamic police work, field duty, transfers frequent, law enforcement pressure. Naib Tehsildar: Level 8 (lower pay), land revenue administration, office-based initially, less physical risk, powerful role in property disputes. DSP offers more action and career advancement; Naib Tehsildar offers stability. Choose: Enjoy field policing? → DSP. Prefer revenue/administrative work? → Naib Tehsildar.

faqs.question: What's the difference between MPPSC Prelims Paper 1 and Paper 2 strategy?
faqs.answer: Paper 1 (GS): Counts for ranking. With new 3-mark negative marking, only attempt 50-70 questions accurately (achieving 45-60 marks out of 300). Don't attempt all 100. Strategy: Solve questions you're 90%+ confident about. Skip medium-confidence questions. Attempt guesses only on 5-10 questions maximum. Paper 2 (CSAT): Qualifying only (need 33%). Less important. Quick strategy: Solve easy math (30%), easy reasoning (30%), English (30%), skip hard questions. Your Paper 2 score doesn't affect ranking — just crossing 33% suffices. Many toppers score 50-60 in Paper 2 and still get selected because Paper 1 performance is dominant.

faqs.question: How frequent are MPPSC exams? Should I keep applying until selected?
faqs.answer: MPPSC exam frequency is irregular — not annual. Last major notification was January 2026 (155 vacancies for SSE). Before this, exams happened in 2019, 2022 — so roughly 2-3 year gaps. Strategy: Don't wait for next MPPSC notification if it's 2+ years away. Keep preparing and simultaneously apply for other state PSCs: UPPSC, BPSC, if you're in Hindi heartland. Or pursue UPSC CSE parallel. Also prepare for SSC exams (CGL, CHSL) which have more frequent notifications. Having multiple exam options ensures you eventually get a government job. MPPSC selection is not mandatory — good performance in UPPSC or other PSC is equally valuable.

syllabus.subject: General Studies Paper I
syllabus.topics: Indian history, geography, polity, economics. Madhya Pradesh-specific: MP history (Gond kingdom, Bhopal princely state, tribal movements), MP geography (Narmada, Vindhya, Satpura), MP tribal culture (Bhil, Gond), MP government schemes (Ladli Behna, Sikho Kamao)

syllabus.subject: CSAT / General Aptitude (Qualifying)
syllabus.topics: Comprehension, logical reasoning, analytical ability, basic numeracy, data interpretation, decision making

syllabus.subject: Madhya Pradesh Specific GK
syllabus.topics: MP history (Gond kingdom, Bhopal princely state, tribal movements), MP geography (Narmada, Vindhya, Satpura), MP tribal culture (Bhil, Gond), MP government schemes (Ladli Behna, Sikho Kamao)

books.title: Madhya Pradesh State GK
books.author: MP GK by Punekar, Lucent GK + MP special
books.subject: Madhya Pradesh GK

study_plan.tasks: Read NCERTs for History, Geography, Polity. Start Madhya Pradesh state GK book. Study state-specific history, geography, culture, and current schemes. Daily newspaper reading for current affairs.
Laxmikanth Polity, Ramesh Singh Economy. Deep dive into Madhya Pradesh-specific topics — this is what separates State PSC from UPSC. Start answer writing practice for Mains (250 words).
Solve last 10 years MPPSC papers. Weekly full mocks. Focus on Madhya Pradesh current affairs and state government policies. Interview preparation alongside.
